28.reason: [Error: Missing handling for data == root->data. According to the problem, values equal to the root should go to the right subtree, but this code does nothing when data == root->data, causing the node to be lost.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The problem requires that values greater than or equal to the root go to the right subtree, but this code only handles strictly greater values. When data equals root->data, the node is not inserted, causing the BST to miss duplicate values. The condition should be `else` instead of `else if (data > root->data)` to include the case data >= root->data.]

41.reason: [Traversal is preorder (visit current node before children), but the problem requires left-to-right leaf order. However, for leaf-only output, preorder, inorder, or postorder may still yield correct left-to-right leaf sequence in BST. The real issue is the insertion logic above.]
